hero.title: How to use the webapp
hero.description: This page explains the main workflow of the platform, from login to project management and real-time collaboration.

steps.title: 1. Sign in to the platform
steps.text: Log in with email and password or through Google OAuth. After authentication, you will be able to access your personal dashboard.

steps.title: 2. Check the dashboard
steps.text: The dashboard shows your calendar, events, tasks, priorities, and summaries of relevant activities.

steps.title: 3. Select a project
steps.text: From the Projects section, you can browse projects according to their status: TODO, ACTIVE, REVIEW, and COMPLETED.

steps.title: 4. Manage tasks
steps.text: Each project can contain tasks with priority, due date, description, and progress status.

steps.title: 5. Use documents and files
steps.text: The Files section allows you to upload and access materials linked to a project or organization.

steps.title: 6. Collaborate in real time
steps.text: The chat system uses WebSocket to support direct messages, project rooms, notifications, and live communication.

tip.title: Usage tip
tip.text: Use the dashboard as your starting point, then enter the active project to work on tasks, files, documents, and chat. In this way, the webapp works as a small Jira-like system integrated with real-time communication.

hero.description: Here you can access the documentation pages generated from the Markdown files stored inside the public/docs folder.

techDocs.title: Backend README
techDocs.text: Fastify architecture, Prisma, authentication, REST APIs, and data management.

techDocs.title: Frontend README
techDocs.text: React structure, routing, dashboard, components, and API calls.

title.line2: to organize projects, tasks, and teams.

hero.text: FT_TRANSCENDENCE is a platform designed to manage organizations, projects, tasks, documents, calendars, and real-time communication. Its goal is to provide a centralized dashboard where users can monitor their team's work, collaborate on projects, and review activity history.

featuresSection.title: How the platform works
featuresSection.description: The webapp is divided into modules: authentication, user profile, organizations, projects, tasks, documents, calendar, and chat.

features.title: Organizations
features.text: Create and manage organizations, teams, and shared workspaces.

features.title: Projects
features.text: Each organization can contain multiple projects with status, description, tasks, and documents.

features.title: Task board
features.text: Manage tasks with a Jira-like flow: TODO, ACTIVE, REVIEW, and COMPLETED.

features.title: Real-time chat
features.text: Communicate through direct messages and project rooms powered by WebSocket.

features.title: Documents and files
features.text: Upload and access files linked to organizations and projects.

features.title: Personal dashboard
features.text: View profile, calendar, tasks, priorities, events, and activity status.

workflow.title: Login
workflow.text: The user signs in with email and password or through Google OAuth.

workflow.title: Dashboard
workflow.text: View calendar, events, tasks, priorities, and activity summaries.

workflow.title: Projects
workflow.text: Select a project and view tasks, documents, and details.

workflow.title: Collaboration
workflow.text: Use real-time chat, notifications, and shared documents.

cta.title: Do you want to understand the project from a technical point of view?
cta.text: Check the README files dedicated to backend, frontend, WebSocket, and database.

educational.title: 1. 42 Educational Project
educational.content: This application was developed exclusively as an educational project for the 42 network. It is not a commercial product and is not intended for production use. The source code, infrastructure, and services provided are to be considered part of an academic exercise.

data_usage.title: 2. Data Collection and Usage
data_usage.content: The application collects only the data necessary to test the functionalities required by the project subjects (such as name, email, avatar, tasks, and friendship relations). This data is stored in a temporary database purely for evaluation and debugging purposes.

warning.title: 3. Personal Data Warning
warning.content: WARNING: Please strictly DO NOT enter real personal data, passwords used on other services, financial information, or any sensitive data. Use exclusively dummy data or test accounts for the evaluation of the project.

privacy.title: 4. Privacy and Security
privacy.content: As this is a testing environment, data persistence is not guaranteed. All entered data may be deleted at any time without notice due to server resets or Docker infrastructure updates. No data will ever be shared or sold to third parties.
